verdicts.note: Rests on a single self-reported pamphlet record item (mental health professionals in the field [S1]); medium is the ceiling and the +1 magnitude is correctly modest. His prosecutor background is biography, not a stated position, so it does not flip the sign.

No carbon-pricing stance in the dossier, but repeated electrification campaigning [S2][S3] plus the Sierra Club endorsement [S6] support +2 at medium; high would have been overstated and was not claimed.
+1 rather than +2 is right: no elected record [S1][S3], but her pitch is 20-year federal-insider expertise, not outsider renewal.

Direction rests almost entirely on one law-enforcement endorsement (WACOPS [S3]); a single endorsement is not a 'strong endorsement pattern.' The Aurora Avenue first-responder roundtables [S1] are directionally ambiguous and his only stated position ('evidence-based approaches to homelessness') is neutral. This is indirect inference, so confidence belongs at low.
Demask-ICE testimony [S1] is a documented action matching the pole_b masking-ban plank verbatim; +2 medium is aggressive but defensible.
High confidence is earned by the documented cost-reduction record [S6], though the dossier itself hedges that citation as 'per Herald coverage of the finance committee.'

Score is sound. Highlight text 'Endorsements skew toward local mayors and councilmembers rather than legislators, labor, or newspapers' is dossier-derived [S2][S3] but glosses over former U.S. Rep. Brian Baird on his endorser list; mildly editorial framing.

Score is sound. Flag on highlight text: 'The only candidate endorsed by the teachers' union via WEA-PAC' presents as fact what the dossier attributes to her own pamphlet statement and website [S1][S2]; should be attributed ('says she is').
Pure biography (pro bono work [S1]) with no stated policy position; +1 is only tolerable because it is marked low, which the app excludes from alignment math.
Sign check passes: protecting Apple Health/Medicaid within existing structures [S2] is pole_a (strengthen current system); nothing in the dossier suggests single-payer advocacy.

+2 (maximal outsider/renewal) overstates her profile: she is a longtime party-establishment officer (LD32 GOP chair 2023-25, King County GOP vice chair 2025-26 [S1]) and a repeat candidate for this exact seat (2024, ~31% [S3]). Her pitch is anti-one-party-control accountability, not outsider/generational renewal; the scorer's own basis concedes the tension. +1 fits the evidence.
